title: selliguea bakeri
intro: Selliguea bakeri, also known as the Philippine giant polypody, is a type of fern species native to the Philippines. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Selliguea bakeri include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems due to its expectorant properties. In traditional herbal preparations, Selliguea bakeri is often dried and made into tea or infusions to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
